description: Pre-seeded validator catalogs for tools/verify_baseline.py and tools/baseline_orchestrator.py. Each list is the initial seed; user is expected to tune (add/remove ids) as the orchestrator output reveals false-positives or misses.
H5_rationale: Question ids that must carry at least one scripture[] entry with figures including 'anthropomorphism' or 'anthropopathism'. Divine-attribute questions where the canon contains well-known anthropomorphic / anthropopathic passages (e.g. Gen 6:6, Ex 32:14, 1 Sam 15:11, Jonah 3:10, Jer 18:8, anthropomorphic 'hand of the LORD' / 'eyes of the LORD' passages, anthropopathic wrath/grief language). A subagent that produces a verdict on these without surfacing the figurative complicating texts is hiding the hermeneutical commitment. Validator failure mode: 'anthropomorphism-required-not-cited' if neither figure appears in any scripture[] entry AND the flag 'anthropomorphic-passages-omitted' is not set.
H6_rationale: Question ids where any verdict that affirms (in the dispensational direction) MUST carry the flag 'dispensational-lens-required' AND have 'dispensationalism' in evidence.hermeneutics.frameworks_in_play. These are eschatology questions where the verdict shifts based on dispensational vs covenantal vs historic-premillennial lens. The flag forces the dispensational commitment (a Brethren distinctive originating with Darby) to be visible, not smuggled. Validator failure mode: 'dispensational-lens-flag-missing' if affirms=true on these ids without the flag.
K2_rationale: Question ids where cult_marker_if_denied=true is plausibly defensible from apparatus + interlinear + concordance. These are doctrines whose denial Scripture itself signals as gospel-implicating (Galatians 1:8-9 anathema, 1 John 4:1-3 spirit-of-antichrist, 2 John 7-11 do-not-receive): Trinity boundaries, full deity and humanity of Christ, bodily resurrection (Christ's and general), bodily second coming, virgin birth, eternal sonship, hypostatic union, Christ as exclusive way (pluralism denial), apostolic-canon-closure (no post-apostolic new revelation on par with Scripture), basic monotheism. The orchestrator MUST still verify each on its own per-question canonical evidence. Being on this list grants eligibility to set cult_marker_if_denied=true; it is NOT a free pass. Each question still needs (i) would_die_for=true and (ii) canonical demonstration: pan-canonical lexical reading via apparatus + interlinear + concordance (not from a single passage). Lineage agreement does NOT factor in; Scripture's clarity drives the bar. Validator failure mode: 'cult-marker-on-non-eligible-question' if cult_marker_if_denied=true on an id NOT in this list. NOTE: this catalog and the prompt-level 2-condition bar are intentionally belt-and-suspenders. The prompt instructs the subagent to apply the bar honestly; this catalog is the orchestrator-side allow-list. If a subagent's verdict disagrees with this catalog in practice, that disagreement is itself diagnostic and worth surfacing to the user for catalog re-curation, not silently overridden.
K2_excluded_rationale: Question ids whose stems carry confessional-distinctive framing or theory-specific vocabulary such that canonical demonstration is contested at the lexical level. Lineage agreement is not the issue; lexical settlement is. Excluded clusters: (A) Reformed Solas as worded (sola_fide, sola_gratia, imputed_righteousness, justification_forensic) embed specific theory-of-mechanism that James 2 and Pauline justification language (Rom 4-5 vs Jas 2) lexically complicate; (B) Atonement-theory specificity (penal_substitution as theory-of-mechanism; substitution simpliciter is on the eligible list); (C) Scripture-quality scope-of-claim cluster (final_authority, infallibility, sufficiency, Chicago-scope inerrancy, the Protestant 66-book canon, deuterocanon-not-Scripture) carries scope-of-claim or canon-boundary wording the lexical anchors do not unambiguously license at the precise scope claimed; (D) Denial-of-Catholic-or-Orthodox-distinctive propositions (cult-icc-baptismal-regeneration, het-rc-marian-dogmas, het-icon-veneration) rest on inferential rather than pan-canonical lexical reading; (E) doc-tradition-not-on-par-with-scripture (split from doc-ongoing-revelation-binding) faces 2 Thess 2:15 paradosis directly. These ids may carry would_die_for=true under the owner's reading, but their stems as currently worded fail condition 2 of the cult-marker bar (canonical demonstration) and cannot legitimately carry cult_marker_if_denied=true.
verifier_url_rationale: Two allow-lists (primary repositories + counter-witness primaries) and one deny-list. The verify_baseline.py URL check (--check urls) HEAD-requests every URL in evidence.web[] and reports failures. The deny-list is checked separately: any URL in evidence.web[] containing one of the forbidden domains is a hard validation failure (Reformed-aligned commentary sites that share the formation's substrate, plus brethrenarchive.org which would be defendant material in the baseline).
